name: lift
description: A measure for association rules that compares the confidence to the baseline frequency of the consequent, indicating whether the rule provides improvement over random chance.

name: generalized association rules
description: Extension of association rules to include quantitative and categorical features beyond binary transactions, often by discretization.

name: unsupervised as supervised learning
description: A framework for unsupervised learning problems like clustering and association rules where a supervised learning model is used to predict the presence or absence of features.

name: cluster analysis
description: Methods for finding groups or clusters in data such that observations within each group are more similar to each other than to observations in other groups.

name: data segmentation
description: Another name for cluster analysis, the process of grouping data into meaningful subsets.

name: proximity matrix
description: An N × N matrix accumulated in random forests where for every tree, any pair of out-of-bag observations sharing a terminal node has their proximity increased by one, used to visualize similarity between observations.

name: dissimilarity
description: A measure of how different two observations are, typically satisfying d(xi, xi) = 0 and d(xi, xi′) ≥ 0.

name: attribute dissimilarity
description: Dissimilarity measures based on the attributes or features of observations, such as Euclidean distance for quantitative variables.

name: gower dissimilarity
description: A general dissimilarity coefficient for mixed data types (quantitative, ordinal, and categorical) that combines different attribute-specific dissimilarities.

name: object dissimilarity
description: Dissimilarity measures that treat observations as unstructured objects without explicit attributes, measured directly between observations.

name: combinatorial clustering algorithms
description: Clustering algorithms that work directly with observed data or dissimilarities and assign observations to clusters.

name: mixture modeling clustering
description: A probabilistic clustering approach that assumes data is generated from a mixture of underlying probability distributions.

name: mode seeking clustering
description: Clustering methods that attempt to directly estimate distinct modes of the probability density function, with each mode representing a cluster.

name: hierarchical clustering
description: Clustering methods that produce a sequence of nested partitions from fine (all singletons) to coarse (all observations in one cluster), often represented as a dendrogram.

name: dendrogram
description: A tree diagram representing the hierarchical clustering structure showing how observations and clusters are merged or split at different levels.

name: agglomerative clustering
description: A bottom-up hierarchical clustering approach that starts with each observation as its own cluster and successively merges closest pairs.

name: divisive clustering
description: Top-down hierarchical clustering starting with all data in one cluster, recursively splitting clusters to maximize between-group dissimilarity.

name: single linkage
description: A dissimilarity measure between two groups defined as the minimum dissimilarity between any observation in one group and any observation in the other.

name: complete linkage
description: A dissimilarity measure between two groups defined as the maximum dissimilarity between any observation in one group and any observation in the other.

name: average linkage
description: A dissimilarity measure between two groups defined as the average dissimilarity between all pairs of observations from the two groups.

name: Ward's method
description: An agglomerative hierarchical clustering method that minimizes the within-cluster sum of squares at each merge, similar to K-means objective.

name: vector quantization
description: Image and signal compression technique using K-means clustering to approximate blocks of pixels by cluster centroids (codewords), with applications in lossy compression.

name: codeword
description: The cluster centroid used to approximate a block of pixels in vector quantization; the collection of codewords forms the codebook.

name: encoding step
description: In vector quantization, the clustering process that assigns each data block to its nearest codeword.

name: rate-distortion tradeoff
description: In lossy compression, the tradeoff between compression rate (bits per pixel) and distortion (reconstruction error).

name: variable length coding
description: A coding scheme that assigns shorter codes to more frequent codewords, using entropy-based Shannon coding theory.

name: entropy of codeword distribution
description: The Shannon entropy of the distribution of codewords in variable length coding, determining the optimal compression rate.

name: tree-structured vector quantization
description: A generalization of VQ that finds centroids with a top-down, 2-means style algorithm, allowing successive refinement of compression.

name: k-medoids clustering
description: Clustering method restricting cluster centers to actual observations, minimizing total dissimilarity to cluster members using arbitrary dissimilarity measures.

name: medoid
description: The observation in a class with smallest average distance to other observations in that class, used in classification and k-medoids clustering.
section: 18.5.2 Classiﬁcation and Other Models Using Inner-Product

name: silhouette coefficient
description: A measure of clustering quality that compares within-cluster tightness to between-cluster separation for each observation.

name: gap statistic
description: Method for estimating optimal number of clusters by comparing log WK curve to that from uniformly distributed data, finding where gap is largest.

name: self-organizing map
description: A constrained version of K-means clustering where prototypes lie in a low-dimensional manifold (usually 1D or 2D grid), preserving topological relationships.

name: principal curves
description: Smooth one-dimensional curves that pass through the middle of a distribution, generalizing principal components to nonlinear manifolds.

name: principal surfaces
description: Smooth two-dimensional surfaces that generalize principal curves to higher-dimensional manifolds.

name: spectral clustering
description: A clustering method that uses the eigenvectors of the graph Laplacian matrix to embed data in a lower-dimensional space where clusters are more easily separated.

name: graph laplacian
description: A matrix representation of a graph defined as L = G - W where G is the diagonal degree matrix and W is the adjacency matrix, used in spectral clustering.

name: similarity graph
description: A graph representation of data where nodes are observations and edges represent similarities, used in spectral clustering.

name: kernel density estimation
description: A nonparametric method for estimating the probability density function by placing kernel functions at each data point.

name: encoding step in vector quantization
description: The clustering process in vector quantization where pixel blocks are assigned to their nearest codeword.

name: rate in bits per pixel
description: Measure of compression efficiency expressed as log2(K)/4 bits per pixel, where K is the number of codewords.

name: fixed-length code
description: Coding scheme using the same number of bits for each codeword, requiring log2(K) bits to identify K codewords.

name: variable length code
description: Coding scheme using different numbers of bits for different codewords based on frequency, achieving better compression via Shannon coding theory.

name: cophenetic correlation coefficient
description: Correlation between pairwise observation dissimilarities and their cophenetic dissimilarities from the dendrogram, measuring how well hierarchy represents data.

name: cophenetic dissimilarity
description: Intergroup dissimilarity at which two observations are first joined together in the same cluster in hierarchical clustering.

name: ultrametric inequality
description: Constraint on cophenetic dissimilarities requiring Cik ≤ max(Cii′, Ci′k) for any three observations, implying isosceles triangles.

name: single linkage agglomerative clustering
description: Hierarchical clustering taking intergroup dissimilarity as minimum dissimilarity between groups; tends to chain clusters via intermediate observations.

name: complete linkage agglomerative clustering
description: Hierarchical clustering taking intergroup dissimilarity as maximum dissimilarity between groups; produces compact clusters with small diameters.

name: group average clustering
description: Hierarchical clustering using average dissimilarity between groups; compromise between single and complete linkage, attempts compact and well-separated clusters.

name: cluster diameter
description: Largest dissimilarity among members of a cluster, measuring cluster compactness.

name: batch som
description: Batch version of self-organizing maps processing all observations together rather than one at a time.

name: neighborhood function in som
description: Function h giving more weight to prototypes closer in topological coordinate space, used in sophisticated SOM updates.

name: reconstruction error in clustering
description: Sum of squared distances ||x − mj||² between observations and their assigned prototypes, measuring clustering quality.

name: term-document matrix
description: Matrix representation where each row is a document and entries are relative frequencies of predefined terms, used for document retrieval.

name: principal component analysis
description: Sequence of best linear approximations to data of all ranks q ≤ p, providing uncorrelated projections ordered by variance.

name: rank-q linear model
description: Parametric representation of affine hyperplane f(λ) = μ + Vqλ approximating data points by least squares.

name: right singular vectors
description: Columns vj of matrix V in SVD, defining principal component directions; first q columns solve rank-q approximation problem.

name: singular values
description: Diagonal elements d1 ≥ d2 ≥ ⋅⋅⋅ ≥ dp ≥ 0 of diagonal matrix D in SVD, measuring variance along principal component directions.

name: procrustes transformation
description: Finding optimal rotation R and location μ to match two sets of corresponding points by minimizing ||X2 − X1R − 1μ^T||F.

name: procrustes average
description: Shape M minimizing average squared Procrustes distance to collection of L shapes, found by alternating algorithm.

name: principal curve
description: Smooth one-dimensional curved approximation to data satisfying self-consistency: f(λ) = E(X|λf(X) = λ), generalizing principal component line.

name: self-consistency property
description: Property of principal curves where f(λ) equals the average of all data points projecting to it.

name: principal surface
description: Higher-dimensional generalization of principal curves providing curved manifold approximation, commonly used in two dimensions.

name: mutual k-nearest-neighbor graph
description: Similarity graph connecting pairs where one point is among K-nearest neighbors of the other, with nonzero edge weights only for neighbors.

name: normalized graph laplacian
description: Standardized version of Laplacian with respect to node degrees, e.g., L̃ = I − G^(-1)W.

name: kernel principal components
description: Nonlinear PCA obtained by applying PCA in implicit feature space defined by kernel, expanding scope to non-linear transformations.

name: sparse principal components
description: PCA variant using L1 penalties to encourage sparse loadings, making principal components easier to interpret.

name: scotlass
description: Sparse PCA method maximizing variance subject to L1 constraint on loadings: max v^T(X^TX)v subject to ∑|vj| ≤ t, v^Tv = 1.

name: elastic net for sparse pca
description: Sparse PCA using regression/reconstruction property with ridge and lasso penalties, allowing efficient computation via alternating optimization.

name: non-negative matrix factorization
description: Alternative to PCA approximating X ≈ WH where all elements are non-negative, useful for modeling non-negative data like images.

name: archetypal analysis
description: Method approximating data points by convex combinations of prototypes (archetypes) that themselves are convex combinations of data, forcing prototypes onto convex hull.

name: factor analysis
description: Classical latent variable model X = AS + ε where S are uncorrelated factors, modeling correlation structure with separate disturbances ε.

name: independent component analysis
description: Latent variable model X = AS where components S are statistically independent (not just uncorrelated), exploiting non-Gaussianity for identifiability.

name: blind source separation
description: Recovery of independent source signals from mixed observations, classical application of ICA in cocktail party problem.

name: differential entropy
description: Entropy H(Y) = −∫g(y)log g(y)dy measuring randomness of continuous variable; Gaussian has maximum entropy for fixed variance.

name: mutual information
description: Kullback-Leibler distance between joint density and product of marginals, measuring dependence between components of random vector.

name: negentropy
description: Non-negative measure J(Yj) = H(Zj) − H(Yj) quantifying departure from Gaussianity, where Zj is Gaussian with same variance as Yj.

name: exploratory projection pursuit
description: Graphical exploration technique for visualizing high-dimensional data by finding non-Gaussian projections revealing interesting structure like clusters or tails.

name: projection indices
description: Measures focusing on different departures from Gaussianity for optimization in projection pursuit, similar to negentropy.

name: tilted gaussian density
description: Representation fj(sj) = φ(sj)e^(gj(sj)) for departures from Gaussianity, used in product density ICA approach.

name: prodeniica
description: ICA algorithm estimating product density directly using generalized additive models with penalized Poisson log-likelihood and smoothing splines.

name: amari metric
description: Measure of closeness between two frames (orthogonal mixing matrices), used to compare estimated and true mixing matrices in ICA.

name: multidimensional scaling
description: Finding low-dimensional representation zi ∈ R^k preserving pairwise dissimilarities dij, requiring only dissimilarities not data points.

name: kruskal-shephard scaling
description: Least squares MDS minimizing stress function SM = ∑i≠i′(dii′ − ||zi − zi′||)².

name: sammon mapping
description: MDS variation minimizing weighted stress putting more emphasis on preserving smaller pairwise distances.

name: classical scaling
description: MDS minimizing ∑i,i′(sii′ − ⟨zi, zi′⟩)² for similarities sii′, with explicit eigenvector solution; equivalent to PCA for centered inner products.

name: metric scaling
description: MDS methods (least squares and classical scaling) approximating actual dissimilarities or similarities rather than just ranks.

name: shephard-kruskal nonmetric scaling
description: MDS using only ranks, minimizing stress over zi and arbitrary increasing function θ via isotonic regression.

name: isotonic regression
description: Method finding best monotonic approximation θ(dii′) to ||zi − zi′|| with zi fixed in nonmetric scaling.

name: isometric feature mapping
description: Nonlinear dimension reduction constructing graph to approximate geodesic distance along manifold via shortest path, then applying classical scaling.

name: local linear embedding
description: Nonlinear dimension reduction preserving local affine structure by approximating points as linear combinations of neighbors in both high and low dimensions.

name: geodesic distance on manifold
description: Distance along manifold surface approximated by shortest path through neighbor graph in ISOMAP.

name: pagerank algorithm
description: An algorithm used by Google to rank webpages, considering a webpage important if many other important webpages point to it, with recursive weighting based on the PageRank of linking pages and their number of outgoing links.

name: random forest
description: A substantial modification of bagging that builds a large collection of de-correlated trees by randomly selecting a subset of variables at each split, and then averages them.

name: out-of-bag samples
description: A validation technique in random forests where each observation's prediction is made using only trees from bootstrap samples in which that observation did not appear, providing a built-in cross-validation estimate.

name: out-of-bag error
description: An error estimate in random forests that is almost identical to N-fold cross-validation, computed using out-of-bag samples.
